page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6149 processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate moderate multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 7203.49 and a PassMark CPU score of 8280.79, both placing in the average tier. Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth reaches 402.4 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write speeds are 38.98 GB/sec and 33.47 GB/sec respectively. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1091.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1355.68. The instance demonstrates average performance in software compilation and Redis SET operations, but struggles with resource-intensive tasks such as medium-model LLM inference and database operations.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, the ecs.hfc5.2xlarge offers a balanced compute-focused profile for workloads that require consistent CPU performance without massive memory footprints. It is suitable for tasks such as software compilation, text processing, and moderate web serving. However, the lack of local storage and lower single-core benchmark results make it less optimal for high-performance database systems or memory-intensive data science applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 6149 x86_64 processor clocked at 3.1 GHz. It is configured with 16.0 GB of RAM, translating to 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 8280.79 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.08 million operations per second, though single-core metrics and memory scores fall into the lower tier. This hardware profile is best suited for parallelizable, compute-focused workloads such as software compilation, text processing, and basic web serving, where dedicated CPU execution is required but massive memory or local storage is not.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 16 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suitable for software compilation and text processing.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core Intel Xeon compute-optimized virtual server configured for parallel processing and moderate web serving workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core performance for software compilation and Redis operations
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for compute-focused workloads, text processing, and web serving
